key_insight: LLMSurvival turns censored time-to-event prediction into pairwise ranking, allowing unmodified LLMs to learn survival risk directly from tabular clinical data.
engineering_relevance: The framework shows that censoring-aware learning can be embedded into a general-purpose foundation model pipeline, offering a portable alternative to hand-crafted risk scores and classical survival models.
method: Reformulate survival prediction as comparisons among comparable patient pairs.
Train unmodified LLMs on tabular clinical features using censoring-aware pairwise ranking.
Estimate test-time risk by aggregating comparisons against anchor subjects from the training cohort.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on ICU mortality in MIMIC-IV and fragility fracture prediction in a NewYork-Presbyterian/Weill Cornell cohort, LLMSurvival outperformed Cox PH and three deep survival models, with gains reported in concordance.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of anchor comparisons, and the approach still needs careful validation before deployment across new clinical settings.
paper_title: Towards end-to-end LLM-based censoring-aware survival analysis
